scenarios.title: Medical Imaging and Segmentation Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Computational pathology, whole-slide image analysis, medical image segmentation, tissue masks, stain normalization, pathology model inference, annotation stores, and visualization overlays.
scenarios.role: Use `tiatoolbox` for TIAToolbox package workflows in digital pathology: WSI I/O, preprocessing, model inference, annotation storage/querying, visualization, and CLI planning.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions TIAToolbox, TIA Toolbox, tiatoolbox, WSIReader, PatchPredictor, SemanticSegmentor, MultiTaskSegmentor, AnnotationStore, stain-norm, tissue-mask, whole-slide pathology images, or pathology visualization overlays.
scenarios.best_for: Using TIAToolbox APIs or CLI to read WSIs, prepare image patches and masks, run pretrained/custom pathology inference, inspect annotation outputs, visualize overlays, and troubleshoot installation/backend/configuration issues.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is generic Python repo maintenance, Docker/release automation, benchmark execution, or unrelated medical imaging packages such as MONAI, nnU-Net, or TorchIO.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose TIAToolbox when the request is about computational pathology or the named TIAToolbox APIs/commands. Use broader computer-vision or medical-imaging skills only when the package is not TIAToolbox or the task is about a different framework.

scenarios.title: Computer Vision Modeling and Augmentation
scenarios.when_to_read: Computer vision segmentation, classification, image preprocessing, stain augmentation, feature extraction, and model-output visualization in pathology images.
scenarios.role: Use `tiatoolbox` when a vision request specifically names TIAToolbox or pathology-image APIs rather than a generic CV framework.
scenarios.read_when: User asks for TIAToolbox stain normalization, tissue masking, patch extraction, image augmentation, pathology segmentation, nucleus detection, or model output overlays.
scenarios.best_for: Pathology-specific CV workflows that combine image I/O, preprocessing, pretrained model engines, annotation formats, and visualization.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use a generic computer-vision skill for YOLO/SAM/OpenMMLab/TorchVision requests that do not involve TIAToolbox or pathology WSI workflows.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Prefer the medical-imaging scenario for WSI or pathology tasks; use this secondary scenario only when the user frames TIAToolbox as a computer-vision preprocessing or model-inference library.
